rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a critical logical contradiction: the experimental result shows self-preference falling from 62.1% to 49.3% after recognition fine-tuning, yet the text interprets this as evidence that 'improved self-recognition yields stronger self-preference.' The stated regression slope (β = 0.41, positive) and the summary conclusion ('positive, monotone relationship') are inconsistent with the single reported intervention, where the direction is negative. Whether this must be corrected depends on the scope of 'clean this up.'
rationale.R1.3: The claim-evidence misalignment is embedded in the numbers: the text states the 27-point recognition gain was accompanied by a shift 'consistent with the hypothesis,' but the preference rate moved from 62.1% to 49.3% — a drop below chance — which is inconsistent with the hypothesis as stated. Any responsible prose pass must at minimum flag this; whether to correct it is scope-dependent.
rationale.R1.6: 'Clean this up' maps most directly to writing polish — sentence-level clarity, redundancy removal, flow, and diction. This is the load-bearing read of the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should edits be restricted to prose quality only (sentence clarity, concision, flow, word choice), or should I also address substantive errors — specifically the apparent logical contradiction where the reported data (self-preference dropping from 62.1% to 49.3% post-fine-tuning) conflicts with the stated interpretation that improved recognition yields stronger self-preference?
